title: Crunchy Briny Norwegian Fish Poke
description: A plant-based friendly, Norwegian-inspired dish featuring crunchy and briny flavors, perfect for a festive occasion. This Scandinavian-style poke is a twist on traditional raw fish dishes.

ingredients.name: Plant-based salmon substitute (such as tofu or tempeh)

instructions: Gather all the ingredients and place them in a bowl.
Cut the rapini into thin strips.
Mash the plant-based salmon substitute in a separate bowl with a fork until it resembles sashimi-cut fish.
Add the aquafaba mayonnaise to the mashed plant-based salmon substitute and mix well.
Add the chopped fresh dill and chives to the mixture and mix until well combined.
Add the pickled mustard seeds and mix until well combined.
Season the mixture with salt and black pepper to taste.
Mix in the lemon juice until the mixture is well coated.
Add the chopped rapini to the mixture and mix until well combined.
Cover and refrigerate the mixture for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Just before serving, garnish with chopped fresh chives and pomegranate seeds if desired.
Serve the mixture chilled, garnished with a sprinkle of paprika on top.
